transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for calling Sunrise Recovery Center, this is Leonard. How can I help you today?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Hi, I'm Brandon. I've been struggling with benzodiazepines — Xanax, mostly — for about two years. I want to get help but I'm scared about what people will think. I'm a teacher.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for calling, Brandon. That took courage. The fear about what people will think — especially in a profession like teaching where your reputation matters — is something a lot of people face. I want to walk you through the privacy picture, because it's actually more protective than most people realise.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Your treatment is completely confidential under HIPAA — your employer, your school district, anyone you haven't specifically authorised cannot access your records. Your insurance company processes the claim but does not share diagnosis information with your employer. If you need time off for treatment, FMLA protects your job and HR is only told that you're on approved medical leave — not why. Does that help?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes. A lot. I didn't know about FMLA.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Most people don't. And for teachers specifically — there are programs designed around schedules, including summer intensive options and evening outpatient. Benzo dependence is also something our medical team has a lot of experience with because it does require a supervised taper. Have you been taking them as prescribed, or are you taking more than prescribed?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Both at this point. Started prescribed, now I'm taking more than I should.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I understand. That's a really common progression. The supervised taper is important because stopping benzodiazepines abruptly can be medically dangerous — I want to flag that for our clinical team. Brandon, do you have health insurance?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes, through the school district. It's BluePeak Health. Member ID BP46039549, group GRP-429686.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you. District plans often have good mental health and substance use coverage. I'll have our benefits team verify. They'll be discreet — any calls will show up as Sunrise Recovery Center on caller ID, not as a specific clinic type, if that matters to you.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: It does. Thank you for thinking of that.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Of course. Here's what happens next: benefits verification within ninety minutes, followed by a call from our intake team who will talk through level of care and scheduling with your work calendar in mind. They'll call from our main line — just Sunrise Recovery Center. Does two o'clock work for you?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect. Brandon — teachers reach out for help less often than they should because of exactly the fear you named. I'm glad you called.

gold_labels.coaching_notes: Excellent stigma objection handling — agent proactively offered the FMLA/HIPAA detail without being asked, and thought to mention the caller ID display (shows treatment center name, not clinic type). Clinical screen correctly flagged benzo taper risk, which is medically important. The summer program / teacher schedule detail showed genuine attentiveness to the caller's situation. Near-flawless call.
